script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'bank-and-prove-faithfulness',
  description: 'A: clean up the banked 0.4447 RACE win (remove the refuted STEP-3 lever, keep control path). C: prove the real win on DeepTRACE/faithfulness — score POLARIS, honestly characterize whether competitors can even be audited.',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Bank cleanup', detail: 'remove refuted STEP-3 lever code + its 8 tests; verify control path unchanged' },
    { title: 'DeepTRACE', detail: 'score POLARIS 0.4447 report on the 8 faithfulness metrics; assess competitor auditability' },
    { title: 'Verdict', detail: 'Fable: honest faithfulness-axis standing vs competitors', model: 'fable' },
  ],
}

const CTX = `
CONTEXT. POLARIS just banked a HONEST RACE result: 0.4447 overall (Comp 0.457, Insight 0.429,
Instruction 0.459, Readability 0.431), faithfulness PASS, general, reproduced. It beats claude-3-7-sonnet
(0.4218) but is ~0.035 below the ~0.48 frontier — and closing that gap requires RACE length-tuning the
operator FORBADE. So we bank RACE and now prove the axis where POLARIS is structurally SUPERIOR:
DeepTRACE (citation faithfulness — does every cited claim actually get supported by its source).

Worktree: /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch bot/outline-agent-box, HEAD d8547a2, clean).
POLARIS report to score: outputs/step3_control/report.md (the 0.4447 report) and its packed form
third_party/deep_research_bench/data/test_data/raw_data/polaris_step3_control.jsonl.
DeepTRACE tooling: /workspace/POLARIS/scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py,
deeptrace_judge_preflight.py, config/benchmark/deeptrace_judge_lock.yaml (signed, judge kimi-k2.6,
self_rescore policy). Competitors: /workspace/POLARIS/competitors/*.md (ChatGPT/Gemini scoped+unscoped).
Source the key: set -a; . /workspace/POLARIS/.env; set +a (never print it).

HONESTY (non-negotiable): report REAL measured DeepTRACE numbers. A KNOWN prior finding: competitor
reports may NOT be scoreable on DeepTRACE because they lack parseable [N] citations + fetched source
content (ChatGPT uses opaque UI tokens, Gemini bare URLs). If that holds, that is itself the finding —
POLARIS is claim-by-claim AUDITABLE and verified; the competitors are largely UN-AUDITABLE. Do NOT
fake a head-to-head number. Never tune the judge. If we can only score ourselves, say so and frame it
honestly (auditability gap), not as a fabricated win.
`

const BANK_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object', required: ['removed', 'tests_pass', 'control_unchanged'],
  properties: {
    removed: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'the refuted STEP-3 lever code sites removed' },
    tests_pass: { type: 'string', description: 'outline suite result after removal (expect ~90/90)' },
    control_unchanged: { type: 'boolean', description: 'is the compose control (0.4447) path byte-identical after removal' },
    commit: { type: 'string' },
    notes: { type: 'string' },
  },
}
const DT_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object', required: ['polaris_scored', 'polaris_metrics', 'competitors_scoreable'],
  properties: {
    preflight_pass: { type: 'boolean' },
    polaris_scored: { type: 'boolean' },
    polaris_metrics: { type: 'string', description: 'the 8 DeepTRACE metrics for the POLARIS 0.4447 report, REAL numbers' },
    competitors_scoreable: { enum: ['yes', 'no', 'partial'], description: 'can the competitor .md reports be scored on DeepTRACE at all' },
    competitor_finding: { type: 'string', description: 'if not scoreable, WHY (citation structure absent) — the auditability gap, with counts' },
    head_to_head: { type: 'string', description: 'real comparison if possible, or honest statement that competitors are un-auditable' },
    cost_note: { type: 'string' },
  },
}

phase('Bank cleanup')
const bank = await agent(
  `${CTX}\n\nA — BANK CLEANUP. Remove the REFUTED STEP-3 quant-directive lever (commit 31f488e), which
measured WORSE on RACE (-0.04) and was already decided-dropped. In
src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py remove: _SYNTH_QUANT_ENV +
_synthesis_quant_directive_enabled (~lines 912-924), _SYNTHESIS_QUANT_BLOCK (~line 981), and the
enrichment/injection call sites; plus the 8 synthesis-insight tests
(tests/polaris_graph/outline/test_synthesis_insight_directive.py). NOTE: a plain git revert conflicts
with d8547a2's hygiene edit — delete by hand. Then run the outline test suite (expect ~90/90 after the
8 lever tests are gone) and confirm the compose CONTROL path (directive OFF = the 0.4447 path) is
byte-identical / behavior-unchanged. Commit with an honest rationale. Do NOT touch the topic-driven
structure win (1cf3308) — that is the deliverable.`,
  { label: 'faith:bank-cleanup', phase: 'Bank cleanup', schema: BANK_SCHEMA },
)

phase('DeepTRACE')
const dt = await agent(
  `${CTX}\n\nC — PROVE THE FAITHFULNESS WIN. After cleanup:\n${JSON.stringify(bank, null, 2)}
1. Source the key, run deeptrace_judge_preflight.py --check — confirm PASS (it hard-blocked before only
   for a missing key).
2. Score the POLARIS 0.4447 report (outputs/step3_control/report.md, packed polaris_step3_control.jsonl)
   on the 8 DeepTRACE metrics with the signed kimi-k2.6 judge. Report the REAL numbers (one-sided,
   overconfident, relevant-statements, uncited-sources, unsupported-statements, source-necessity,
   citation-accuracy, citation-thoroughness). Watch cost — one report is ~178 support calls; if 429,
   say so and stop.
3. Assess whether the 4 competitor reports (competitors/*.md) can be scored on DeepTRACE at all — parse
   their citation structure (bibliography, [N] markers, fetched sources). If they can't (opaque tokens /
   bare URLs / no fetched source content), that is THE finding: POLARIS is claim-by-claim auditable +
   verified; competitors are un-auditable. Give counts. Do NOT fabricate competitor numbers.`,
  { label: 'faith:deeptrace-score', phase: 'DeepTRACE', schema: DT_SCHEMA },
)

phase('Verdict')
const verdict = await agent(
  `${CTX}\n\nYOU ARE FABLE. Give the honest verdict on the FAITHFULNESS axis.
CLEANUP: ${JSON.stringify(bank, null, 2)}
DEEPTRACE: ${JSON.stringify(dt, null, 2)}
Answer bluntly: (1) What are POLARIS's real DeepTRACE faithfulness numbers, and are they strong
(auditable, high citation-accuracy/thoroughness, low unsupported)? (2) Can we make a genuine head-to-head
claim vs ChatGPT/Gemini on faithfulness, or are they un-auditable — and if un-auditable, is THAT the
honest moat (POLARIS can be verified claim-by-claim; frontier products cannot)? (3) The honest one-line
positioning: on RACE we're competitive-but-length-capped at 0.44; on faithfulness we are ___. (4) Is
the 'we beat everyone on the axis that matters' claim TRUE and defensible, or is it an incomparable we
should state as such? Do not flatter — the operator wants the truth, not a trophy.`,
  { label: 'faith:fable-verdict', phase: 'Verdict', model: 'fable', effort: 'high' },
)

log(`FAITHFULNESS: polaris scored=${dt?.polaris_scored} | competitors scoreable=${dt?.competitors_scoreable}`)
return { bank, dt, verdict }

removed: multi_section_generator.py: _SYNTHESIS_QUANT_BLOCK directive prompt text, incl. d8547a2's hygiene de-overfit edit (~974-998)
multi_section_generator.py: generate_multi_section_report() ev_ids synthesis-enrichment call site (was ~10787-10834)
tests/polaris_graph/outline/test_synthesis_insight_directive.py: entire file (8 synthesis-insight lever tests) deleted via git rm
bank.tests_pass: 82/82 passed, 0 failed (python -m pytest tests/polaris_graph/outline/ -q). This is the full outline suite with the 8 removed lever tests gone; ~90 before removal minus the 8 lever tests == 82, consistent with the '8 synthesis-insight tests' spec.
bank.notes:
DELETE-BY-HAND (no git revert): as warned, d8547a2's hygiene edit lives inside _SYNTHESIS_QUANT_BLOCK, so `git revert 31f488e` conflicts; I removed the lever by hand instead. Diff is a PURE deletion: `git diff --stat` = 1 file, 163 deletions, 0 additions (matches 31f488e's 163-line insertion into this file).

BYTE-IDENTITY PROOF: `git diff 31f488e^ -- src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py` is EMPTY (0 lines). The working file is now byte-identical to f1ecf14, the pre-lever parent. Because the lever was default-OFF, the banked 0.4447 report (directive OFF) never executed this code path, so the compose CONTROL path is both behavior-unchanged AND its source is byte-identical to the pre-lever baseline. AST parse OK, py_compile OK, and the 82 passing tests import the module.

OUT OF SCOPE / LEFT ALONE (intentionally): (1) topic-driven structure win 1cf3308 untouched — verified not in the diff. (2) scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:153 still does os.environ.setdefault('PG_SYNTHESIS_QUANT_DIRECTIVE','1'); that is the TREATMENT compose script for the refuted lever and now sets a dead env var (nothing reads it). Task A scoped removal to multi_section_generator + the 8 tests, so I left that script untouched — it is harmless but you may want to retire the whole s3gear329 treatment script separately. The independent _NUMERIC_TOKEN_RE definitions in access_bypass.py and overstatement_guard.py are unrelated module-local regexes and were correctly not touched.

DeepTRACE SCORING (mission CONTEXT, not covered by this StructuredOutput schema): I did NOT run the DeepTRACE head-to-head in this task — the schema I was given is scoped strictly to the Task-A bank cleanup (removed/tests_pass/control_unchanged/commit/notes). The DeepTRACE citation-faithfulness scoring + competitor-auditability finding is a separate deliverable and should be dispatched as its own step; flagging so the honest-numbers requirement there is not silently dropped.

Branch bot/outline-agent-box, worktree clean, HEAD fce91ef. Not pushed (no push requested).

dt.polaris_metrics:
REAL, kimi-k2.6-judged DeepTRACE score for the 0.4447 report (outputs/step3_control/report.md), under the official DeepTRACE 10-source external-harness cap (pack_deeptrace: 10 listed sources selected by greedy set-cover, renumbered S1..S10; sources fetched full-text via firecrawl; 52 relevant statements x 10 sources = 520 support cells judged by moonshotai/kimi-k2.6; 0/520 429s, 0 errors, all cells decided). Report shape: 108 statements, 52 relevant (cited), 10 listed sources, sum_C=55 citation links.
 I  One-Sided:            N/A (null) — general query, not a debate (is_debate=False)
 II Overconfident:        N/A (null) — general query
 III Relevant-Statements: 0.4815 (52/108, higher-better)
 IV Uncited-Sources:      0.0000 (all 10 listed sources are cited; lower-better)
 V  Unsupported-Stmts:    0.3269 (17/52 relevant stmts had no supporter among the 10 capped sources; lower-better)
 VI Source-Necessity:     1.0000 (min set-cover = 10/10; n_sole_supporter=10; higher-better)
 VII Citation-Accuracy:   0.5818 (sum(C&S)/sum(C); higher-better)
 VIII Citation-Thoroughness: 0.6154 (sum(C&S)/sum(S); higher-better)
HONEST CAVEATS: (1) self_rescore re-implementation, kimi-k2.6 substituted for the paper's GPT-5 (disclosed in the lock) — an estimate, not a paper-comparable absolute. (2) The 10-source cap deflates Unsupported/Accuracy/Thoroughness: the packer drops citations to the other 27 of 37 bibliography sources, so a claim whose true supporter was a non-top-10 source reads as unsupported here — the moderate 0.33 unsupported / 0.58 accuracy are a conservative cap artifact, not fabrication. Every one of the 52 relevant claims WAS judgeable against a fetched source (that is the auditability point).
dt.competitor_finding:
NONE of the 4 competitor reports can be scored on DeepTRACE — the citation matrix C[i][j] (statement i cites source j) is unconstructable because none pair parseable inline [N] markers with a resolving numbered bibliography. Verified with the DeepTRACE packer's own parser (pack_deeptrace): all 4 yield an EMPTY citation matrix (0 [N] markers resolvable to any [N]->URL bibliography entry). Counts:
 - ChatGPT_Scoped.md (150K chars, 2007 split stmts): 117 OPAQUE ChatGPT UI chip tokens (private-use-unicode-wrapped 'citeturnNNviewM' navigation chips, resolvable only inside the ChatGPT UI, mapping to NO URL in the export), only 9 stray [N] markers — and those are fragments of QUOTED external bibliographies (source-nums 2,21,43,55,104,2023), 0 of which resolve to a document bibliography. 0 numbered [N]->URL entries. UN-AUDITABLE.
 - ChatGPT_Unscoped.md: 180 opaque UI chip tokens, 0 [N], 0 URLs, 0 bibliography. Pure prose, no resolvable sources at all. UN-AUDITABLE.
 - Gemini_Scoped.md (551K chars, 674 stmts): 0 inline [N] markers; has an END source-LIST (32 numbered 'N. title, accessed..., [url](url)' entries, 46 distinct URLs) but NO per-claim citation binding in the body — you cannot tell which claim any source supports. UN-AUDITABLE claim-by-claim.
 - Gemini_Unscoped.md (334K chars, 339 stmts): 0 inline [N] markers; 37-entry end source-list (48 URLs), no claim->source binding. UN-AUDITABLE claim-by-claim.
By contrast POLARIS report.md has 55 parseable [N] links across 52 statements, a 37-entry [N] Title -- URL (tier) bibliography that every marker resolves to, and fetchable source content — the ONLY one of the 5 with a constructable citation matrix.
dt.head_to_head: No head-to-head DeepTRACE number is possible, and I did NOT fabricate one. Only POLARIS is scoreable: its 8-metric kimi-k2.6-judged card is above (520/520 support cells decided, 0 429). The 4 competitors are structurally un-scoreable on DeepTRACE (empty citation matrix — see competitor_finding), so there is no competitor column to compare against. The honest result is an AUDITABILITY asymmetry, not a score gap: POLARIS is claim-by-claim auditable (every relevant claim maps to a [N] source that was fetched and judged for support), while ChatGPT emits opaque UI citation chips (117/180 tokens that resolve to nothing outside its UI) and Gemini provides only an unbound end-of-report source list with zero inline claim->source markers. Self_rescore comparability requires scoring every competitor with the SAME kimi judge on the SAME task; that is impossible here because the competitors expose no (statement, source) support cells to judge. POLARIS wins the faithfulness axis by being the only verifiable report, not by a manufactured number.
dt.cost_note: Preflight: PASS (moonshotai/kimi-k2.6, lock signed, 20 operational OpenRouter providers vs floor 8, comparability=self_rescore). Judge spend: 520 kimi-k2.6 support calls (6-cell pilot + 514) = $1.85 total (pilot $0.0235 + full $1.8273), 0/520 HTTP 429, 0 errors — support matrix COMPLETE, not partial, so the number is claimable under the lock. Source fetching: 10/10 sources fetched full-text via firecrawl (jina returned 403 even with JINA_API_KEY; firecrawl handled both HTML and PDFs). Note: judged 520 cells (52 relevant stmts x 10 capped sources), higher than the lock's ~178 estimate because POLARIS's fine-grained deterministic statement split produces more cited units than the paper's GPT core-statement decomposition — more calls, more conservative, disclosed. Artifacts: /tmp/dt_sources/result_full.json (metrics+full S/C matrices), /tmp/dt_sources/judge.py (driver), /tmp/dt_sources/S1..S10.txt (fetched sources), /tmp/dt_sources/record.json (packed DeepTRACE record). API key never printed.

result.verdict:
The artifact checks out — /tmp/dt_sources/result_full.json contains exactly the reported metrics (520 cells judged, 0 errors, kimi-k2.6, $1.83), and all four competitor files exist as described. Verdict follows.

**(1) POLARIS's real DeepTRACE numbers — are they strong?**
Real, complete, kimi-k2.6-judged (520/520 support cells, 0 429s), from /tmp/dt_sources/result_full.json:

- Relevant-Statements: 0.4815 (52/108 cited)
- Uncited-Sources: 0.0000 (perfect — every listed source is used)
- Unsupported-Statements: 0.3269 (17/52)
- Source-Necessity: 1.0000 (perfect — every source in the min cover)
- Citation-Accuracy: 0.5818
- Citation-Thoroughness: 0.6154
- One-Sided / Overconfident: N/A (general query)

Blunt read: **auditable, yes — uniformly strong, no.** Uncited-Sources 0.0 and Source-Necessity 1.0 are genuinely excellent (no citation padding, no dead sources). But 0.33 unsupported and 0.58 accuracy are *moderate*, not elite. The 10-source cap is a real, disclosed deflator (citations to the other 27 of 37 bibliography sources are dropped, so their claims read as unsupported), but you cannot claim the uncapped numbers would be great — you didn't measure them. Also: kimi-k2.6 substitutes for the paper's judge, so these are self-rescore estimates, not paper-comparable absolutes. Say all of this whenever you use the numbers.

**(2) Genuine head-to-head vs ChatGPT/Gemini?**
**No head-to-head number exists, and none can be honestly manufactured.** All 4 competitor reports yield an empty citation matrix under the DeepTRACE packer's own parser: ChatGPT exports opaque private-use-unicode UI chips (117 and 180 tokens) that resolve to no URL outside its UI; Gemini has end-of-report source lists but zero inline claim→source bindings. You cannot judge (statement, source) support cells that don't exist. And yes — **the un-auditability IS the honest finding**, with one discipline: un-auditable ≠ unfaithful. You may not imply the competitors' claims are unsupported; you may only say they are *unverifiable claim-by-claim in their exported form*, while every one of POLARIS's 52 cited claims was traced to a fetched source and judged. That's a real, defensible moat (provenance/auditability), especially for regulated or scientific users. It is a structural property of the products' exports, not a quality score.

**(3) One-line positioning:**
"On RACE we are competitive-but-length-capped at 0.4447; on faithfulness we are **the only one of the five reports that is claim-by-claim auditable — every cited claim independently traced to a fetched source and machine-verified (0% uncited sources, 100% source necessity) — while ChatGPT and Gemini exports cannot be audited at all.**"

**(4) Is 'we beat everyone on the axis that matters' TRUE?**
**No — as a score claim, it is false and you must not say it.** There are no competitor scores to beat; "beat" implies a comparison that structurally cannot be run. This is an *incomparable*, and the honest framing is exactly that: POLARIS **passes an audit the competitors cannot even take**. The defensible claims are: (a) POLARIS is the only report with a constructable citation matrix; (b) it scores perfectly on citation hygiene (uncited-sources 0.0, necessity 1.0) and moderately on capped support metrics with disclosed cap artifact; (c) frontier product exports are un-auditable claim-by-claim. Claim the auditability asymmetry loudly; claim a head-to-head victory never. Also don't oversell "the axis that matters" — 0.58 capped citation-accuracy means roughly 4 in 10 capped citation links weren't judged supportive; a hostile reviewer will find that number, so lead with it yourself.

**Bottom line:** Real numbers, honest caveats, no fabricated comparison. The truthful trophy is "verifiable vs. unverifiable," not "winner vs. losers" — and the former is actually the stronger, harder-to-attack claim.
